Front: What is internal energy?
Back: The total kinetic and potential energy of particles in a system

Front: What is temperature?
Back: A measure of the average kinetic energy of particles

Front: What is absolute zero?
Back: The lowest possible temperature where particles have minimum energy

Front: What is absolute zero in Kelvin
Back: 0 K

Front: What is the relationship between Celsius and Kelvin
Back: Kelvin equals Celsius plus 273

Front: What is specific heat capacity?
Back: Energy needed to raise temperature of 1 kg by 1 degree

Front: What is the formula for specific heat capacity
Back: Energy equals mass times specific heat capacity times temperature change

Front: What is the unit of specific heat capacity
Back: Joules per kilogram per Kelvin

Front: What is specific latent heat?
Back: Energy needed to change state without temperature change

Front: What is the formula for latent heat
Back: Energy equals mass times specific latent heat

Front: What is the unit of latent heat
Back: Joules per kilogram

Front: What is latent heat of fusion?
Back: Energy to change solid to liquid

Front: What is latent heat of vaporisation?
Back: Energy to change liquid to gas

Front: What happens during change of state
Back: Temperature remains constant

Front: What is thermal equilibrium?
Back: When two objects are at same temperature

Front: What is conduction?
Back: Heat transfer through solids

Front: What is convection?
Back: Heat transfer by movement of fluids

Front: What is radiation?
Back: Heat transfer by electromagnetic waves

Front: What type of waves transfer thermal radiation
Back: Infrared waves

Front: What is a perfect black body?
Back: An object that absorbs all radiation

Front: What is emission of radiation?
Back: Transfer of energy away from surface

Front: What affects rate of heat transfer
Back: Surface area temperature difference material

Front: What is insulation?
Back: Reducing heat transfer

Front: What is the kinetic theory of gases?
Back: Model describing gas particles in motion

Front: What assumptions are made in kinetic theory
Back: Particles are small random and collide elastically

Front: What is pressure in a gas?
Back: Force per unit area from particle collisions

Front: What is the formula for pressure
Back: Pressure equals force divided by area

Front: What is the unit of pressure
Back: Pascal Pa

Front: What is Boyle Law?
Back: Pressure inversely proportional to volume at constant temperature

Front: What is Charles Law?
Back: Volume proportional to temperature at constant pressure

Front: What is pressure law?
Back: Pressure proportional to temperature at constant volume

Front: What is the ideal gas equation
Back: Pressure times volume equals number of moles times gas constant times temperature

Front: What is the symbol for gas constant
Back: R

Front: What is the unit of gas constant
Back: Joules per mole per Kelvin

Front: What is number of moles?
Back: Amount of substance

Front: What is the formula for moles
Back: Mass divided by molar mass

Front: What is root mean square speed?
Back: Measure of average particle speed

Front: What affects kinetic energy of gas particles
Back: Temperature

Front: What happens when temperature increases
Back: Particles move faster

Front: What is internal energy change
Back: Due to heating or work done

Front: What is first law of thermodynamics
Back: Change in internal energy equals energy added minus work done

Front: What is work done by a gas
Back: Energy transferred when gas expands

Front: What happens during compression
Back: Work done on gas increases energy

Front: What happens during expansion
Back: Gas does work on surroundings

Front: What is isothermal process
Back: Temperature remains constant

Front: What is adiabatic process
Back: No heat transfer

Front: What is pressure volume graph
Back: Graph showing gas behaviour

Front: What does area under PV graph represent
Back: Work done

Front: What is thermal conductivity
Back: Measure of how well a material conducts heat

Front: What is thermal insulation example
Back: Double glazing loft insulation

Front: What is specific heat capacity practical
Back: Heating known mass and measuring temperature change

Front: What is latent heat practical
Back: Measuring energy during state change

Front: What is Brownian motion?
Back: Random movement of particles in fluid

Front: What causes Brownian motion
Back: Collisions with molecules

Front: What is diffusion?
Back: Movement of particles from high to low concentration

Front: What affects diffusion rate
Back: Temperature particle mass medium

Front: What is density of a gas
Back: Mass per unit volume

Front: What is pressure in terms of particles
Back: Collisions with container walls

Front: What is cooling curve
Back: Graph showing temperature decrease over time

Front: What is heating curve
Back: Graph showing temperature increase over time

Front: What happens at flat sections of heating curve
Back: Change of state

Front: What is thermal energy transfer
Back: Movement of energy due to temperature difference

Front: What is equilibrium in gases
Back: Equal energy distribution

Front: What is kinetic energy formula for particles
Back: Half times mass times speed squared

Front: What is mean kinetic energy
Back: Average energy of particles

Front: What is internal energy in solids
Back: Vibrations of particles

Front: What is internal energy in gases
Back: Kinetic energy of moving particles

Front: What is the effect of pressure increase
Back: Particles collide more frequently

Front: What is effect of volume decrease
Back: Pressure increases

Front: What is effect of temperature on pressure
Back: Higher temperature increases pressure

Front: What is gas constant equation rearranged
Back: Pressure times volume divided by temperature equals constant

Front: What is real gas?
Back: Gas that does not follow ideal behaviour

Front: What causes deviation from ideal gas
Back: Intermolecular forces and volume of particles

Front: What is energy transfer in conduction
Back: Vibration and electron movement

Front: What is convection current
Back: Flow of fluid due to density differences

Front: What is Stefan Boltzmann law
Back: Power radiated proportional to temperature to the fourth power

Front: What is emissivity
Back: Measure of how well a surface emits radiation

Front: What is specific latent heat of vaporisation
Back: Energy to convert liquid to gas per kg

Front: What is specific latent heat of fusion
Back: Energy to convert solid to liquid per kg
